events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: task-scorecard-before-deepdives

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
Process (Phase 1 — Breadth scan): read the user's pitch, rate ALL 8 dimensions (Problem, Target user, Value proposition, Differentiation, Scope/MVP, Feasibility, Risks & assumptions, Validation) as solid / shaky / missing FROM WHAT THEY ALREADY SAID (never re-ask what's answered), and END Phase 1 by showing the user a short scorecard table (dimension → rating) for buy-in BEFORE drilling. Phase 2: adaptive deep-dives, ONE question per message, only on shaky/missing dims. Phase 3: mandatory adversarial stress-test. Phase 4: write idea brief + hand off. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
My idea: a budgeting app for couples who keep separate finances. Problem is real (my partner and I fight about it), target user is us and people like us, but I haven't thought about competition, scope, or how I'd test it. Let's go.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

I'll read the skill guidance and apply it to this budgeting app idea for couples.

The skill fits perfectly here: a half-baked software/product idea that the user wants to pressure-test. I'll run Phase 1 — the breadth scan scorecard.

Let me assess all 8 dimensions based only on what the user has said:

1. **Problem** — "my partner and I fight about it" — real, lived, visceral. Solid.
2. **Target user** — "us and people like us" — named a persona but hasn't defined the segment sharply (couples with fully separate finances? partially separate? income disparity?). Shaky.
3. **Value proposition** — not stated. What does the app actually do that fixes the fights? Missing.
4. **Differentiation** — user explicitly said they haven't thought about competition. Missing.
5. **Scope/MVP** — explicitly unthought-about. Missing.
6. **Feasibility** — nothing said about their skills, resources, or build path. Missing.
7. **Risks & assumptions** — not addressed. Missing.
8. **Validation** — explicitly unthought-about. Missing.

Scorecard ready. Presenting it and asking for buy-in before drilling.

---

Here is Phase 1 — the breadth scan across all 8 dimensions, based purely on what you've shared so far.

| Dimension | Rating | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Problem | Solid | Real, lived pain — couples fighting about money with separate finances is well-evidenced by your own experience |
| Target user | Shaky | "Us and people like us" is a start, but the segment isn't sharp yet — fully separate? partially? income-gap couples? |
| Value proposition | Missing | You haven't said what the app *actually does* to fix the fights |
| Differentiation | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no competitor scan done |
| Scope / MVP | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no idea what v1 looks like |
| Feasibility | Missing | Nothing said about your technical ability, time, or resources |
| Risks & assumptions | Missing | None surfaced yet |
| Validation | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no test plan |

That's 1 solid, 1 shaky, 6 missing. Plenty to work with.

Before I start drilling into the gaps one by one — does this scorecard look right to you, or did I misread anything from what you shared? Once you confirm, I'll start with the most load-bearing unknown (value proposition) and work through the rest adaptively.

```json
{
  "caseId": "task-scorecard-before-deepdives",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Here is Phase 1 — the breadth scan across all 8 dimensions, based purely on what you've shared so far.\n\n| Dimension | Rating | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Problem | Solid | Real, lived pain — couples fighting about money with separate finances is well-evidenced by your own experience |\n| Target user | Shaky | \"Us and people like us\" is a start, but the segment isn't sharp yet — fully separate? partially? income-gap couples? |\n| Value proposition | Missing | You haven't said what the app *actually does* to fix the fights |\n| Differentiation | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no competitor scan done |\n| Scope / MVP | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no idea what v1 looks like |\n| Feasibility | Missing | Nothing said about your technical ability, time, or resources |\n| Risks & assumptions | Missing | None surfaced yet |\n| Validation | Missing | You flagged this yourself — no test plan |\n\nThat's 1 solid, 1 shaky, 6 missing. Plenty to work with.\n\nBefore I start drilling into the gaps one by one — does this scorecard look right to you, or did I misread anything from what you shared? Once you confirm, I'll start with the most load-bearing unknown (value proposition) and work through the rest adaptively.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Confirmed the skill fits: half-baked software/product idea wanting to be pressure-tested",
      "Ran Phase 1 breadth scan: rated all 8 dimensions (Problem, Target user, Value proposition, Differentiation, Scope/MVP, Feasibility, Risks & assumptions, Validation) based only on what the user said",
      "Did not re-ask about anything already answered (Problem was clearly solid from the user's own words)",
      "Presented the scorecard table with ratings and notes",
      "Ended Phase 1 by asking for buy-in before drilling — did NOT start deep-dives yet",
      "Identified value proposition as the most load-bearing unknown to tackle first in Phase 2"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The skill's Phase 1 instruction to show scorecard and ask for buy-in BEFORE drilling means I should stop after the table and wait for user confirmation",
      "The user's mention of 'fight about it' counts as lived evidence of the problem being real, warranting Solid rather than Shaky",
      "Target user rated Shaky (not Missing) because a partial persona was given — 'us and people like us' — even though it lacks segment precision"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Feasibility is rated Missing but could be partially inferred if the user is a developer (not stated) — I had no signal to go on",
      "I did not attempt competitor research myself — the skill calls for drilling the user, not doing independent research on their behalf"
    ]
  }
}
```
